'''Lachlan Miller''' (born 14 August 1994) is an Australian [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]].

==Playing career==

===Rugby sevens===
Miller was a member of the Australian men's rugby sevens' squad at the [[Tokyo 2020 Olympics]].<ref>{{cite web |url=https://olympics.com/tokyo-2020/olympic-games/en/results/rugby-sevens/results-men-gpa-000200-.htm |title=Rugby Sevens - Australia vs Argentina - Pool A Results |work=Tokyo 2020 |access-date=31 July 2021 |archive-date=31 July 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210731021458/https://olympics.com/tokyo-2020/olympic-games//en/results/rugby-sevens/results-men-gpa-000200-.htm |url-status=dead }}</ref> The team came third in their pool round and then lost to Fiji 19-nil in the quarterfinal.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Australian Olympic Team for Tokyo 2021 |url=https://www.theroar.com.au/olympics/australian-olympic-team/ |access-date=6 April 2022 |website=The Roar |language=en-US}}</ref>

===2022===
In round 11 of the [[2022 NRL season]], Miller made his first grade debut for Cronulla and scored a try in the clubs 25-18 victory over the [[Gold Coast Titans|Gold Coast]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2022-05-22/nrl-rabbitohs-raiders-titans-sharks/101089470/|title=Cronulla Sharks score 25-18 NRL win over Gold Coast as Canberra defeats South Sydney 32-12|website=www.abc.net.au}}</ref>
In round 21 of the season, Miller came off the bench and played in the centres, replacing [[Siosifa Talakai]] in the Sharks 24-18 win over the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|Dragons]]. In this game he managed a game high 6 tackle breaks.
Miller played for Cronulla in their elimination final loss to [[South Sydney Rabbitohs|South Sydney]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/cronullas-dual-gamble-pays-off-major-flaw-exposed-in-finals-disaster-brutal-review/news-story/619a3957ee64720ef66e0e9542f375b7/|title=Young gun who can solve Sharks' big problem; masterstroke that can't be overlooked: Brutal Review|website=www.foxsports.com.au}}</ref>

===2023===
In January, Miller was released from his Cronulla contract to sign a three-year deal with the [[Newcastle Knights]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2023/01/31/knights-secure-lachlan-miller/|title=Knights secure Lachlan Miller|date=31 January 2023|website=Newcastle Knights}}</ref>

In round 1 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Miller made his Newcastle debut in the club's 12-20 loss to the [[New Zealand Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-live-newcastle-knights-vs-new-zealand-warriors-live-scores-highlights-supercoach-live-team-lists-kalyn-ponga-warriors-recruits-signings/news-story/c97130d1157437d83818cdc1d7d75a4e/|title='Back on home soil': CNK's 'outstanding' return, Knights 'massive' Ponga blow — Big Hits|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=3 March 2023 }}</ref> In round 3, Miller scored two tries for Newcastle in their 36-20 loss against the [[Dolphins (NRL)|Dolphins]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.abc.net.au/news/2023-03-17/nrl-knights-dolphins-roosters-rabbitohs/102097528/|title=Sydney Roosters triumph over Rabbitohs 20-18 in tense NRL battle, Dolphins stay unbeaten with 36-20 win over Newcastle Knights|newspaper=ABC News|date=17 March 2023 }}</ref> Miller served as Newcastle’s primary fullback to start the season, and after five rounds he led the league in tackle-busts and was top-five in average run metres.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2023-04-11 |title='Could've been digging holes': Inside the gamble that led to ex-rugby star's staggering NRL rise |url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-lachie-miller-newcastle-knights-contract-cronulla-sharks-craig-fitzgibbon-rugby-7s-olympics/news-story/cf1c90571662a9cd44a64777e8409499 |access-date=2023-06-12 |website=Fox Sports |language=en}}</ref> Despite his career-best form to start to the season, Miller later struggled to find consistency and by round 13 was relegated to a bench role as Newcastle star [[Kalyn Ponga]] returned to his familiar fullback position.<ref>{{Cite web |title=NRL team lists round 13 2023 {{!}} Newcastle Knights bench Lachie Miller, Kalyn Ponga to return to fullback role |url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-team-lists-round-13-2023-newcastle-knights-axe-lachie-miller-kalyn-ponga-to-fullback-20230523-p5j56c.html |access-date=2023-06-12 |website=amp.nine.com.au}}</ref>

On 23 August, Miller was released from the final two years of his Newcastle contract, so he could sign a three-year deal to join English side [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] starting in 2024.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-2023-nrl-transfer-centre-rugby-league-news-rumours-reaction-lachie-miller-tanah-boyd-canberra-raiders-tom-starling/news-story/9b15067398398a73faa87351be30c7c3/|title=Flyer's new home after tumultuous season; Titans extend halfback - Transfer Centre|website=www.foxsports.com.au|date=23 August 2023 }}</ref>

===2024===
Miller played 26 matches for Leeds in the 2024 Super League season and scored seven tries as the club finished 8th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>

===2025===
Miller played 25 games for Leeds in the 2025 Super League season including the clubs heart breaking 16-14 loss against St Helens in the elimination playoff match.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce86r25lrxno/|title=Saints snatch stunning play-off victory at Leeds|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
